title: 5 takeaways from the 2026 Oscar nominations, where 'Sinners' made history
text: When the 2026 Oscar nominations were announced, "Sinners" made an unprecedented mark by receiving 16 nominations—more than any film in Academy Awards history. The film's nomination in Best Original Screenplay for Ryan Coogler was among the major categories recognizing the film. Key Takeaways: The historical significance of "Sinners" receiving the most nominations ever set the stage for what would become a landmark Oscars ceremony. Ryan Coogler's nomination for Best Original Screenplay highlighted the screenplay's strength and cultural impact. The film also received nominations for Best Picture, Best Director (Coogler), Best Actor (Michael B. Jordan), Best Cinematography (Autumn Durald Arkapaw), and Best Original Score (Ludwig Göransson), among many others. The nomination run showcased how a single film could dominate multiple categories across acting, technical, and creative achievements. "Sinners" entered the ceremony as a major contender in virtually every significant category. The film's record-breaking nomination count signaled a potential historic night for the horror-thriller genre at the Academy Awards.
